name: Nancy's Best Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons butter. Drizzle in a design on top of the cheesecake. Chill until set.
recipeInstructions:
Combine dry ingredients and softened butter until well mixed. Add egg yolk and vanilla extract and blend thoroughly.
Dust your fingers with flour and pat dough onto the bottom and up about 1 inch (2.5 cm) on the sides of a 10-inch (25 cm) cheesecake pan. Use a 10-inch Kaiser springform pan with a non-stick finish.
Filling:
Beat cream cheese in a large mixing bowl until smooth and creamy.
Combine dry ingredients; stir into the cream cheese mixture. Add eggs and beat just until combined (do not overbeat). Stir in the chocolate, sour cream, and vanilla extract, mixing until combined. Pour into crust-lined pan.
Bake at 325 degrees Fahrenheit for 60-70 minutes or until the center appears set or cracks. Remove from oven and let cool for 15 minutes. Run a spatula around the edge of the pan to loosen the cheesecake. Cool for an additional 30 minutes. Remove sides of the pan, cover, and chill at least 2 hours.
Top:
Melt semi-sweet chocolate and butter. Drizzle in a lattice design on top of the cheesecake. Chill until set. Makes 12 servings. Store in the refrigerator.
Note: The crust can be used for any cheesecake; if it's used on a New York-style or plain cheesecake, add 1 teaspoon grated orange or lemon zest. A chocolate crust can also be made by finely crushing 1 1/2 cups chocolate wafers and mixing with 6 tablespoons melted butter. Pat into the pan.
